multiplechoiceresponse.label: Which of the following best describes the effect of the transformation \(Y_i^{\prime}=Y_i\left(1-2 Z_i\right)\) on the training process?

choicegroup.choice: It smooths labels to reduce variance
It changes the feature values associated with an observation
It allows outcome labels to be conditionally flipped during optimization
It enforces demographic parity automatically, without constraints

div.p: The transformation explicitly changes the outcome label when \(Z_i=1\). This enables the optimization model to choose which labels to flip in order to meet fairness constraints. The lecture makes clear that demographic parity is achieved by jointly optimizing model parameters and label flips, not automatically or by changing features. The process is discrete and controlled.
